mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which translates into sustained corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically low unemployment correlates with economic expansion
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5% for 12 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of those months, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation or overheating, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank rate hikes often precede market corrections
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points over any 3-month period, and the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a low unemployment rate signifies robust consumer demand and purchasing power, directly boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% MoM AND UNRATE < 5%
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows 3-month average growth above 0.5% and UNRATE is below 5%, yet the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in housing starts after a period of decline indicates renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating broader economic activity and driving equity markets higher over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST shows 3 consecutive months of MoM growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market recovery often leads broader economic recovery
failure_condition: If HOUST demonstrates 3 consecutive months of MoM growth exceeding 2%, and the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong corporate performance and supporting positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output is a key indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive MoM growth for 6 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of those months, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to investor concerns about future earnings and subsequent negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to central bank tightening
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0% for 3 consecutive months, and the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of those periods, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls alongside robust GDP expansion signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for positive long-term equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 150k AND GDPC1 annual growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Employment and GDP are primary economic growth indicators
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 150k and GDPC1 annual growth is above 2%, yet the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed contraction in real GDP for two consecutive quarters signals a recession, leading to widespread corporate earnings downgrades and a significant decline in S&P 500 valuations as investors price in economic weakness over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 shows negative growth for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:Two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth define a technical recession
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows negative growth for two consecutive quarters, and the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of the subsequent periods, this hypothesis is rejected.
